headline: OpenAI commits to building a fully automated AI researcher.

items.title: OpenAI is throwing everything into building a fully automated researcher
items.summary: OpenAI has announced a major shift in its research focus, aiming to develop an AI researcher capable of autonomously addressing large, complex challenges. This ambitious project involves creating an agent-based system that can independently conduct research, potentially transforming the landscape of AI development by automating tasks currently requiring human expertise. By leveraging its vast resources, OpenAI aims to push the boundaries of AI capabilities in research and development. The initiative could significantly accelerate AI innovation by reducing the time and labor involved in complex scientific exploration.
items.why_it_matters: This development could drastically enhance the efficiency and scope of AI tool development, impacting how tech leads plan and execute AI projects.

items.title: Build a Domain-Specific Embedding Model in Under a Day
items.summary: Hugging Face has released a guide on leveraging NVIDIA's latest technologies to finetune domain-specific embedding models swiftly. The approach is designed to ease the process of adapting embeddings to niche datasets, enhancing model performance in specific domains. This guide provides step-by-step instructions, enabling developers to achieve significant improvements in model specificity and accuracy within a short timeframe.

items.title: What's New in Mellea 0.4.0 + Granite Libraries Release
items.summary: IBM has released version 0.4.0 of Mellea along with new Granite Libraries, offering improved performance and new functionalities for AI model deployment and management. These updates are designed to enhance model efficiency and expand the libraries' capabilities, catering to a broader range of AI applications. The release includes updates to existing tools and introduces new ones to streamline AI workflows.

all_items.summary: This week I want to look at where we are with psychedelics, the mind-altering substances that have somehow made the leap from counterculture to major focus of clinical research. all_items.title: Show HN: Termcraft – terminal-first 2D sandbox survival in Rust
all_items.summary:
I’ve been building termcraft, a terminal-first 2D sandbox survival game in Rust.

The idea is to take the classic early survival progression and adapt it to a side-on terminal format instead of a tile or pixel-art engine.

all_items.title: Profiling Hacker News users based on their comments
all_items.summary:
Here's a mildly dystopian prompt I've been experimenting with recently: "Profile this user", accompanied by a copy of their last 1,000 comments on Hacker News.

all_items.title: Using Git with coding agents
all_items.summary:
Agentic Engineering Patterns >

Git is a key tool for working with coding agents. Keeping code in version control lets us record how that code changes over time and investigate and reverse any mistakes. All of the coding agents are fluent in using Git's features, both basic and advanced.
